name: Verify all golden harnesses pass
description: Run the full golden harness suite against current fixtures after test/fixtures reorg (ex67ul28). Recipe URL: bun run test:ai:recipe:url:golden (all 4 goldens). Spoonacular: bun run test:ai:spoonacular:ingredient:pick:golden and test:ai:spoonacular:ingredient:pick:batch:golden. Requires CLAUDE_API_KEY, CLAUDE_MODEL, and no Spoonacular key for pick harnesses. Fix any failures; document if a golden is flaky or needs fixture refresh.
result: All golden harnesses pass: recipe URL (5/5), spoonacular ingredient pick (10/10), spoonacular batch pick (2/2). Verified 2026-05-18.

name: Batch pick: Claude helper + Zod schema
description: Implement batched pick helper with Zod schema for N items in one call, keyed by desiredName. Done when module exists and matches single-item pick semantics.
result: pickSpoonacularIngredientCandidatesBatchWithClaude + Zod schema; shared golden-fixture-schemas loader.

name: Storage usage review
description: Check disk usage across all volumes, flag anything over 80%

name: Fallow: severity + baseline/ratchet plan
description: Decide whether rules.unused-dependencies should be warn vs error; decide whether/when to introduce baselines (dead-code/dupes/health) and ratchet over time; document intended steady-state gate.
result: Ratchet via .fallowrc.jsonc: health + dupes baselines for audit; unused-dependencies warn (not error) until manifest is trimmed.

name: Fix ingredient fuzzy match: generic onion → sweet onion
description: Import preview maps generic Onion to Sweet Onion when Sweet Onion exists in DB (Feel Good Foodie Pasta Bolognese: https://feelgoodfoodie.net/recipe/pasta-bolognese/). Likely findBestIngredientMatchByName tokenScore treats single-token onion as substring match on sweet onion. Goal: no auto-link to more-specific variant unless recipe line implies it; prefer not in library yet. Golden: feelgoodfoodie-pasta-bolognese. Code: ingredient-resolution.ts.

name: Networking audit
description: Review and tidy up home network config

name: Slug fuzzy-match: public repos only
description:
Implemented in routing: Levenshtein distance ≤2, unique best match among public projects only. Private repos require exact slug. Revisit threshold/algorithm if too loose or strict.

Related: h39pjguq uses same public filter for / picker.
result: projects.json has private flag from GitHub; routing fuzzy-matches public slugs only (Levenshtein ≤2, unique best).

name: Fallow: deps hygiene (unused + test-only deps)

result: package.json already clean: removed @tanstack/react-router-ssr-query earlier; @cloudflare/vite-plugin, @tailwindcss/vite, drizzle-kit in devDependencies. Deleted stale pnpm-lock.yaml (bun.lock is source of truth). Promoted unused-dependencies to error in .fallowrc.jsonc; fallow --unused-deps (production) clean; fmt/lint/typecheck/test/fallow:audit pass.

name: UI: project management
description: Splash screen when no project is registered: centered 'gdex' title + a single folder picker to select a dex storage directory (the .jsonl dir containing tasks.jsonl, e.g. ~/.dex/task-db/greg.jsonl). No config file picker — gdex manages the config internally. Once at least one project is registered, show a project list with ability to switch active project and add more. Exact layout TBD — we will discuss when we get here.
result: Project management UI: splash registration (config + storage pickers, path validation via plugin-fs), projects sidebar with active selection and add-project flow, placeholder main content. Wired Tauri invoke commands; added plugin-fs; removed unused plugin-opener.

name: Render recipe markdown in app
description:
## Goal
Rendered instructions on preview + detail so Greg can **audit** steps/notes before confirm and read saved recipes comfortably (parent `jvprxjqg`).

## Today
- Markdown in DB; UI is plain `<pre>`. Query has debug raw LLM markdown panel.

## Work
1. Allowlisted markdown renderer (e.g. react-markdown) for read mode on detail; edit stays textarea for now.
2. Same rendering on **query preview** instructions (not only debug panel).
3. Sanitize/allowlist — imported content is external.

## Acceptance
- Lists/headings/steps readable; preview supports confirm/discard decision.
result: Recipe markdown renders in app (instructions, notes, detail view).

name: Demo project: Homelab spring cleaning
description: Synthetic dex demo — greg profile parent task

name: Polish and fixes
description: Ongoing polish, UX tweaks, and small bug fixes for grynthia.cat. Subtasks added incrementally as ideas come in.
result: Polish milestone merged: 7 recipe URL goldens, catalog, fixture reorg, harness verification, Budget Bytes raw-fetch fix (520k). CI gates (fmt/lint/typecheck/test/fallow) pass locally.

name: Dotfiles: prune stale brew packages
result: Removed from backlog—not tracking brew prune in dex.

name: Milestone 4 (optional): Brewfile + brew bundle
description: Move package lists to Brewfile(s) and use brew bundle; keep wrapper for group prompts.
result: Won't do: interactive brews.sh + TASKS registry is sufficient after M1–M3. Brewfile/brew bundle would duplicate maintenance without a regular brew bundle check habit; non-brew steps stay in bash anyway.

name: Fallow: verify CI audit behavior
description: Confirm CI job uses fallow-rs/fallow@v2 command=audit with fetch-depth=0; ensure it behaves as new-only gate (does not fail PRs for inherited repo debt). Optionally adjust workflow inputs (format/comment/annotations) as desired.
result: CI fallow job passes: fallow-rs/fallow@v2 audit with fetch-depth 0, versioned .fallow/dupes-baseline.json and health-baseline.json, .fallowrc.jsonc audit paths. Gates changed files only (no inherited-debt failures on green PRs).

name: Validate project paths before registration
description: Add a validate_project(config_path, storage_path) Tauri command on the Rust side. Two checks: (1) storage directory contains tasks.jsonl (fast filesystem gate); (2) trial dex list --json call with the provided paths succeeds. Return a clear error message to the UI if either fails. UI should call this before add_project so the user gets feedback before anything is saved.
result: Added validate_workspace Rust command that checks for tasks.jsonl presence and runs a trial dex list --json, then wired it into the frontend registration flow before add_workspace

name: File watcher + tasks-changed event
description: Watch .dex/ with notify + notify-debouncer-mini (~30ms window). On change, emit a signal-only Tauri event (no diff payload). Frontend receives the event and re-calls dex list --json to patch its state. No diffing, no backpressure handling, no frontend debounce — keep it simple. Watcher runs for the lifetime of the app process (inherently only active while gdex is open). Future consideration: pause the watcher when the window is not visible/focused to avoid unnecessary work — worth investigating how macOS surfaces window visibility to Tauri before implementing.
result: Added TaskWatcher (notify-debouncer-mini, 30ms debounce) that watches each startup-registered project storage path and emits tasks-changed with project_id.

name: Batch pick: golden harness script
description: Add bun run spoonacular:ingredient:pick:batch:golden. Loads batch manifests, one Claude call per batch, asserts against inherited *.expected.json. Done when both batch cases pass.
result: spoonacular:ingredient:pick:batch:golden passes both batch cases (inherits per-ingredient expected).

name: Surface task ID with quick copy in detail panel
description: The task ID (e.g. 'xrc0mxfb') should be visible and instantly copyable in the task detail panel. Clicking it copies to clipboard — no extra steps. Consider subtle but accessible presentation: small monospace ID near the task name, copy icon or click-to-copy behavior with brief visual feedback (e.g. checkmark flash). This is foundational for the agent instruction snippets feature (oobeapzz) — the ID is what agents need to reference dex tasks.
result: Added a subtle monospace task ID button in the status row of TaskDetailHeader that copies the ID to clipboard on click, showing a '✓ Copied!' label for 1.2 seconds with accent color, then resetting cleanly via a ref-tracked timeout that is cleared on unmount.

name: UI: ingredient name vs amount vs note
description:
## Goal
Preview and saved-recipe views make it **easy to audit** ingredient lines before saying “yes” — supports the human gate in parent `jvprxjqg`.

## Why
Greg needs to spot wrong names, merged notes, and bad qty/unit at a glance. If the preview is muddy, we’ll confirm bad data into Turso.

## Today
- Query + detail: `name — qty unit note` as one gray suffix; notes look like part of the name.

## Work
1. Shared line layout: **name** prominent; **qty + unit** secondary; **note** visually distinct (sub-line / italic — match app).
2. Apply on `/query` preview and `/recipe/$recipeId`.
3. Payload unchanged (name, quantity, unit, note still separate in API).

## Acceptance
- Notes never read as the canonical ingredient name.
- Greg can review a full ingredient list without opening raw JSON/markdown.
result: Shared RecipeIngredientLine: bold name, secondary qty+unit, italic note on query preview and recipe detail.

name: greg dotfiles
description: Umbrella for greg dotfiles repo (stow, brews.sh, dex profile, shell).
result: brews.sh cleanup finished (M1–M3 done, M4 won't-do). No open subtasks under this umbrella.

name: App feature: paste full recipe + URL (alternate query tab)
description:
Goal: add an alternate mode in the real app where a user pastes an entire recipe plus the source URL, and we run the same AI extraction flow.

Requirements:
- UI: new tab/option on the main query surface
- Input: user provides (a) the full recipe text and (b) the URL (used to dedupe / prevent rescans)
- Server: reuse the same extraction pipeline as URL mode, but pageDigest comes from pasted text (normalized/truncated similarly)
- Safety: still enforce URL normalization/dedupe checks; no unauthenticated access

Done when:
- User can choose URL mode or Paste mode
- Paste mode submits {url, pastedText} and produces the same RecipeQueryResult shape
- Existing URL mode behavior unchanged
- Basic fixture-based test/harness exists for pasted text normalization + extraction prompt input (no live network needed)
result: Paste recipe mode on /query: shared Claude extraction via prepareRecipePageDigestFromPastedText; server accepts pastedText; URL vs Paste UI with textarea.

name: Fallow: adopt + manage findings
description:
Current state (already done)
- fallow installed (devDependency): fallow@2.66.1
- package.json: trustedDependencies includes fallow
- .fallowrc.json exists (strict JSON)
- .gitignore includes .fallow/
- CI: .github/workflows/continuous-integration.yaml has job fallow using fallow-rs/fallow@v2 with command=audit and checkout fetch-depth=0

Observed findings (from bun run fallow)
- Dead code: 31 issues (26 unused exports, 1 unused type, 1 unused dependency, 3 test-only production deps)
- Dupes: 10 clone groups (~8.3% dup rate)
- Health: 32 above threshold
- Deps detail (from fallow dead-code --unused-deps):
  - Unused dependency: @tanstack/react-router-ssr-query
  - Test-only production deps (candidate move to devDependencies): @cloudflare/vite-plugin, @tailwindcss/vite, drizzle-kit

Scope / intent
- Primary: verify CI audit behavior and fix safe deps hygiene.
- Do NOT: large refactors for dupes/health yet (defer unless explicitly selected).
- Severity: decide whether unused-dependencies should stay warn vs become error after signal is trusted.

Commands (local)
- bun run fallow
- bunx fallow dead-code --unused-deps
- bun run build
- bun run typecheck
- bun run test

Acceptance criteria
- CI fallow job runs and gates via fallow audit as intended (new-only by default).
- Dependency decisions are made and validated via build/typecheck/test.
- If removing/moving deps: bun run build + typecheck + test still pass.
result: Fallow adoption complete: CI audit + baselines versioned, ratchet plan in .fallowrc.jsonc, dupes/health baselines committed, deps hygiene done. Remaining dupes/complexity grandfathered; optional dedup pass deferred.

name: Fallow: duplication baseline + 2 clone families
description: 1) Save dupes baseline + wire audit.dupesBaseline. 2) Extract shared helpers in routes (ingredient + recipe.index). 3) Extract shared helpers in scripts (recipe-url-golden + spoonacular-ingredient-pick-golden).
result: Committed dupes-baseline.json (9 clone groups grandfathered). Further dedup deferred; new clones on changed files still fail audit.

name: Script/prod parity audit: unify helpers + assurance
description:
Goal: ensure script harnesses and production code paths share the same helpers and that we have confidence prod behavior matches what scripts test.

Scope:
- Inventory helper functions used by scripts (URL fetch/digest, normalization, schema validation, prompt assembly)
- Compare to production entrypoints and ensure a single source of truth (no drift)
- Add lightweight verification harnesses that run without live network where possible (fixtures-based)

Done when:
- Documented map of script->prod helper usage
- Any duplicated logic is refactored to shared helpers (or explicitly justified)
- At least one fixture-based check validates prod helpers produce the same intermediate artifacts as scripts (e.g. pageDigest normalization)
result: Audited script vs prod helper parity. Goldens exercise same Claude helpers as prod (recipe digest+extract, single/batch picks). Batch goldens use 5-item batches aligned with prod chunk size. Accepted out of scope for now: live fetch/Spoonacular search, full enrich E2E, DB resolution, prod fallbacks (goldens target AI behavior only).

name: Try sync-projects-json skill
description:
Verify the sync-projects-json skill and hub data for src/projects.json.

1. Read .cursor/skills/sync-projects-json/SKILL.md and follow it.
2. From the vilos92.com repo root:
   - GitHub MCP (user-github): search_repositories with query user:Vilos92 fork:false; paginate; build project entries per the skill.
   - Update src/projects.json only if serialized output differs from the file; otherwise leave it unchanged.
   - vp check
   - vp test
3. If MCP sync succeeded (file updated or already in sync) and vp check and vp test pass, mark this dex task complete with a short result summary. If any step fails, leave the task open and report the error.

Auth: GitHub MCP configured in Cursor.
result: projects.json in sync (52 projects); vp check and vp test passed.

name: Reorganize golden fixtures out of src/lib
description: Move golden/test fixtures out of src/lib into a dedicated test area (e.g. test/fixtures or tests/fixtures — pick TS/Bun convention). lib/ should stay focused on production helpers, not fixture data. Scope: inventory current fixture paths, propose target layout, migrate paths + imports/scripts, update any CI/harness references. Collaborate on final directory shape before large moves.
result: Moved all recipe URL and Spoonacular golden/candidate static fixtures from src/lib/ai/**/fixtures to test/fixtures/{recipes,spoonacular}. Updated harness scripts, catalog.toml candidateHtml paths, Fallow/Biome HTML exclusions, and docs. typecheck passes.

name: Collapsed sidebar: icon strip mode
description: The projects sidebar stays and already supports collapse/expand (in progress). This task is about making the collapsed state a good experience. When collapsed, the sidebar shows a vertical strip of rounded squares — one per project — each containing a single letter representing the project name. Clicking a square switches the active project (same as the expanded state). A plus button at the bottom adds a new project. Between the plus button and the expand icon, the project squares scroll vertically (invisible scrollbar, no gutter) when the list overflows the window height. The task list sidebar + detail panel layout is already done and not in scope here.
result: Collapsed sidebar: scrollable letter squares (CollapsedProjectList), expand header, plus opens add flow by expanding with register form; hidden scrollbar strip; shared collapse/add icon styles.

name: Prod: batch ingredient picks in app
description:
Wire batched Claude ingredient picks into production enrichment (not golden scripts only).

Architecture (keep layers):
- lib/ai/spoonacular/* — Claude pick helpers + schemas (single + batch); no DB/auth
- lib/spoonacular/ingredient-nutrition.ts — Spoonacular search + pick orchestration
- fn/* — thin server fns (auth, env, DB); call lib/spoonacular only
- routes — call server fns only; no inline AI
- test:ai:* scripts — call same lib/ai helpers as prod

Routing rule (important):
- pickSpoonacularIngredientCandidateWithClaude / single-item orchestration: ONLY when there is exactly 1 ingredient to pick (N === 1).
- 2+ ingredients: always use pickSpoonacularIngredientCandidatesBatchWithClaude (chunk ~5). Do NOT loop single picks for multi-item work.
- Batch failure for N > 1: retry batch or fail per item — do NOT silently fall back to N single Claude calls (that defeats the purpose).

Scope:
- Plural enrich API for multi-ingredient flows (recipe import, bulk resolve)
- Keep tryEnrichNewIngredientNutritionFromSpoonacular for the single-ingredient case (existing ingredient page path)
- Chunk batch picks at ~5; reuse batch helper + aligned system prompt
- No duplicated pick logic in routes/scripts

Done when:
- Prod multi-ingredient paths use batch helper only; single helper only when N === 1
- test:ai golden harnesses still pass
- No new Claude prompts in fn/ or routes/
result: Prod uses pickSpoonacularIngredientsWithClaude (N=1 single, N≥2 batch chunks of 5) from ingredient-nutrition → resolveIngredientsAgainstDb on new recipe ingredients. Single-ingredient page uses tryEnrichNewIngredientNutritionFromSpoonacular (delegates to plural API). No pick loops for multi-item. CI green.

name: Fuzzy-find hub at /
description:
Replace interim / → GitHub profile redirect with a fuzzy-find UI over Greg's GitHub projects.

Requirements:
- GET / — fuzzy search UI; list only public projects from projects.json (private: false)
- Reuse same projects.json as slug redirect task (includes private flag per repo)
- Selecting a project navigates to repo or vilos92.com/slug

Depends on: slug redirect task (shared map + worker).

Done when:

- Interim profile redirect removed
result: GET / serves minimal hub (hub.ts): slug field + Go navigates to /{slug}; worker fuzzy/exact routing unchanged. Public-only fuzzy note on page. Preact styling deferred to qq345m35.

Relevant dex flags available for future filtering: list --all (include completed), list --archived, list --in-progress, list --query "..." (search name/description). DexClient will need to support these flags when filtering is added.

name: gdex
description: Tauri v2 desktop GUI for the dex CLI. v0 goal: read-only visibility into a dex project's tasks — register project paths, browse the task list, drill into subtasks, view task detail. No editing or mutations in v0. Stack: Preact + Vite + vanilla-extract + bun (see repo AGENTS.md).

name: Golden: budgetbytes carbonara + TBFS thanksgiving turkey
description:
Add two recipe URL goldens (catalog + static HTML + expected.json + harness green):

1. https://www.budgetbytes.com/spaghetti-carbonara/
   - Budget Bytes; WPRM-style card, egg/Parmesan carbonara (no cream), bacon + spaghetti
   - Capture HTML (400k truncate), set parseDifficulty + parsingNotes in catalog.toml

2. https://tastesbetterfromscratch.com/easy-no-fuss-thanksgiving-turkey/
   - Tastes Better From Scratch; large bird, herb butter, cavity stuffing, long cook time
   - Same golden workflow under test/fixtures/recipes/

Done when:

- golden/{base}.html + {base}.expected.json with catalogId
- bun run test:ai:recipe:url:golden passes for both (and full suite)

See test/fixtures/recipes/README.md and golden/README.md.
result: Added budgetbytes-spaghetti-carbonara and tastesbetterfromscratch-easy-no-fuss-thanksgiving-turkey goldens (catalog, HTML, expected.json). Fixed raw HTML fetch budget (520k) so late JSON-LD on Budget Bytes is included. All 7 recipe URL goldens pass.

name: Milestone 3: refactor structure + DRY helpers
description: Centralize install helpers; reduce repetition; keep argument handling/help in sync with available tasks.
result: Deduplicated task handling: introduced TASKS registry, generated usage output, unified run_task dispatcher, and made install_everything iterate tasks.

name: homelab
description: Personal homelab and infra upkeep.

name: Investigation: batch ingredient evaluation to reduce LLM cost
description:
Goal: add batched Claude ingredient picks that compose existing per-ingredient goldens (ktdpt2fi), reducing LLM calls without duplicating Spoonacular fixtures.

Prerequisite (done): ktdpt2fi — 10 individual *.spoonacular.json + *.expected.json; spoonacular:ingredient:pick:golden passes.

Approach:
- Individual goldens remain source of truth for candidates and acceptable picks
- Batch fixtures only list member slugs (inheritance); expected derived from each member's *.expected.json
- Target batch size: 5 per case; 2 batch cases from the 10 individuals (mixed difficulty)
- New harness: spoonacular:ingredient:pick:batch:golden (Claude only, no Spoonacular)

Questions to answer:
- Prompt/schema for N independent picks in one structured response
- Failure modes (cross-item poisoning, especially no_match items like ti-leaves)
- Token/cost tradeoffs vs per-item calls
- Go/no-go for production batching

Done when all subtasks complete.
result: Batched ingredient pick investigation complete: inheritance-based batch manifests (2×5), pickSpoonacularIngredientCandidatesBatchWithClaude + schema, test:ai:spoonacular:ingredient:pick:batch:golden passes. Design doc with go/no-go in batches/README.md. Finding: batch prompt must match single-item judgment (belacan case). Prod batching deferred pending go/no-go.

name: Batch pick: golden manifests (2× batch of 5)
description: Add batch fixture manifests referencing existing per-ingredient slugs. Done when: fixtures/golden/batches/ has 2 *.batch.json (5 members each), README documents format, members resolve to *.spoonacular.json.
result: mixed-pantry-5 + hard-and-edge-5 batch manifests; golden README updated.

name: Recipe import: confirm before persist
description:
Curated recipe ingestion: the DB is for **good** data only, not every parse attempt.

## Principle
- **Scan = preview** (Claude reads the page). **Confirm = commit** (writes + enrichment). Until confirm: no Turso food rows, no Spoonacular, no ingredient resolution that mutates state.
- **Human gate:** Greg reviews parsed recipe + ingredients and can say “whoa” — discard, or take the URL to an agent session to add golden fixtures / fix prompts before trying again. Bad parses should become **tests**, not permanent rows.
- **Cost discipline:** Don’t spend Claude or Spoonacular calls on enrichment until the human is happy with the extracted recipe and ingredient list.

## Context (today)
- Extraction calls `resolveIngredientsAgainstDb` during scan (inserts + optional Spoonacular). Recipe row waits for **Create recipe**.
- Food tables were wiped 2026-05-18; fresh start for this workflow.

## Child tasks
Implement confirm-before-persist first (`z3ksyi5w`), then preview/audit UI (`dh34x9uy`), then library UX (source links, markdown, ingredient↔recipes). When extraction quality is wrong, **pause implementation** and work with Greg on goldens — don’t paper over with more auto-insert logic.

## Done when
- Nothing hits DB or Spoonacular until explicit confirm.
- Preview UI makes it easy to judge ingredient lines before commit.
- Subtasks below complete or explicitly deferred with Greg.
result: Import preview: confirm before persist, ingredient lines, source links, markdown audit UI—all shipped and verified in app.

name: brews.sh cleanup
description: Cleanup/refactor scripts/brews.sh for consistency, safety, and maintainability.
result: Done: M1–M3 shipped (bash, safety, TASKS registry). M4 closed as won't-do—keeping interactive brews.sh as single source of truth.

name: Cloudflare + Hono: slug redirects
description:
Deploy vilos92.com to Cloudflare with Hono worker for path-based redirects.

Requirements:
- GET /:slug — 302 to matching GitHub repo from slug map; no UI on slug paths
- GET / — interim 302 to https://github.com/Vilos92 (username is capital V, unlike vilos92.com domain)
- Single slug map (e.g. projects.json) as source of truth for slugs
- Unknown slug → sensible 404

Stack:
- Wrangler + @cloudflare/vite-plugin
- Hono for routing

Out of scope (separate task): fuzzy-find picker at /

Done when:
- wrangler deploy works locally and on Cloudflare
- /known-slug redirects to correct repo
- / redirects to GitHub profile
- unknown slug returns 404
result: Deployed to Cloudflare: Hono worker, projects.json (52 repos, public/private), slug redirects + fuzzysort fuzzy match (public only), / → GitHub profile interim removed when h39 ships.

name: Ingredient goldens: wrap-up + expand to 10
description:
Goal: finish and harden the ingredient testing/golden workflow.

Scope:
- Expand ingredient goldens to 10 total (or document why not)
- Snapshot Spoonacular once per missing fixture (no re-fetching existing fixtures)
- Define/maintain expected allowlists for Claude pick per ingredient
- Ensure commands are documented and repeatable locally

Done when:
- ingredient-queries.json has the final selected set (<=10)
- For each entry: corresponding *.spoonacular.json + *.expected.json exists
- bun run spoonacular:ingredient:pick:golden passes for all
- README documents snapshot vs pick runs and cost implications
result: 10 ingredient goldens: Spoonacular snapshots + expected picks for all entries in ingredient-queries.json; pick golden harness passes; README documents snapshot vs pick and cost. Ti leaves uses empty Spoonacular results + no_match expected.

name: Backups: verify restic snapshots
description: Dry-run restore of last weekly snapshot

name: Tauri commands + project store
description: Dumb #[tauri::command] pass-throughs. App state: registered projects + active id in tauri-plugin-store settings.json. Add/remove/list projects.
result: Added project_store (settings.json) plus Tauri commands for projects and DexClient get_tasks/get_task; wired in lib.rs via commands.rs.

name: Recipes: link to source URL
description:
## Goal
Outbound **source URL** on recipe list + detail so Greg can reopen the original page, compare to the import preview, and feed bad cases into golden work (parent `jvprxjqg`).

## Today
- `sourceUrl` stored and in APIs; not shown in UI.

## Work
- External link on `/recipe/` cards and `/recipe/$recipeId` when present; `rel="noopener noreferrer"`.

## Acceptance
- Imported URL recipes link back to source; no link when absent.
result: RecipeSourceLink on recipe list and detail when sourceUrl is set (noopener noreferrer).

name: Workspace home panel: details + delete
description: When no task is selected (root view of a workspace), the right panel should show workspace details instead of a placeholder message. Display: workspace name, config file path, and storage directory path — all read-only/non-editable. Include a delete workspace action (with confirmation) that removes it from the registry and returns to the splash screen if no other workspaces remain, or switches to another workspace if one exists. Establish 'workspace' as the user-facing term for what the code currently calls 'project'.
result: WorkspaceHomePanel renders workspace name, config path, and storage path when no task is selected, with a two-step delete confirmation that either returns to the splash screen or activates the next workspace depending on how many remain

name: Rename 'project' to 'workspace' throughout
description: Rename the 'project' concept to 'workspace' everywhere: Rust structs (Project -> Workspace), Tauri commands (add_project -> add_workspace, list_projects -> list_workspaces, etc), TypeScript types and API helpers (projectApi.ts -> workspaceApi.ts, Project -> Workspace), store state (projects -> workspaces, activeProjectId -> activeWorkspaceId), UI copy and component names (ProjectSidebar -> WorkspaceSidebar, etc). The rename is purely mechanical — no behavior changes. Rationale: a workspace contains dex tasks which represent real work items; calling the container a project conflicts with how users think about their actual projects.
result: Renamed project→workspace across Rust and TS. Follow-ups: workspace Zod wire schema (config_path/storage_path→camelCase), task detail child list labeled Tasks not Subtasks. check/typecheck pass.

name: grynthia.cat
description: Umbrella for grynthia.cat repo work. Use child tasks for concrete efforts.
result:
Marking this umbrella as complete per request.

What’s complete:
- Recipe URL golden fixtures + Claude harness
- Ingredient Spoonacular snapshot fixtures + Claude candidate-pick golden harness (initial set)
- Local-only runner docs for ingredient goldens

Note:
- Some unrelated follow-on items (e.g. Fallow tasks) remain open but are being moved to new project umbrellas.

name: Fix recipe parse: cuisineandcocktails ahi tuna tacos
description:
Investigate and fix why https://www.cuisineandcocktails.com/ahi-tuna-tacos/ fails or parses poorly in our URL recipe flow.

Done when:
- Entry added to test/fixtures/recipes/catalog.toml (status=golden, parseDifficulty, parsingNotes, goldenFixtureBase)
- Static HTML + *.expected.json under test/fixtures/recipes/golden/ with catalogId pointing at catalog id
- Extraction/prompt fixes as needed so harness passes
- bun run test:ai:recipe:url:golden passes for this case (and ideally full suite)

Harness: bun run test:ai:recipe:url:golden [base-name] for single-case runs. See test/fixtures/recipes/README.md and golden/README.md for capture workflow.
result: Done. Prompt: omit zero/missing cookTime when JSON-LD has prep+total only; fresh/frozen in note not name. Golden: catalog + HTML + expected.json for cuisineandcocktails-ahi-tuna-tacos. Verified: bun run test:ai:recipe:url:golden — all 5 passed (latest re-run ~83s).

name: UI: task detail
description: Read-only panel showing detail for a selected task (name, description, status, result, children, blockers, etc). No mutation affordances in v0 — this is a viewer only. Exact layout TBD — we will discuss when we get here.
result: TaskDetail read-only panel (name, status, description/result/blocked-by, subtasks). Wired in ProjectMain with master-detail layout: list selects detail; subtasks in detail drive zoom. Breadcrumb navigateToTask syncs list+detail. Per-column scroll (detail + project sidebar body). Collapsible project sidebar with panel icon toggle.

name: Agent instruction snippets: design + implementation
description: The cornerstone feature of gdex: surfacing quick copy/paste instructions for agents, tied to dex operations. Before any implementation, work with the user to design this from scratch — what to show (what instructions, for which dex operations), where to surface them (in the task list? detail panel? a dedicated view?), when to show them (contextually based on task status? always available?), and how they should look and behave. Nothing is prescribed here — this is a collaborative design session first, implementation second. The goal is to make gdex genuinely useful as a companion to agent-driven workflows.

name: Project registration: explicit config + storage paths
description:
Registering a dex project in gdex binds to an existing dex profile (same as CLI/gdex):

- config_path — user-chosen dex project config file (e.g. ~/.dex/projects/greg/config.toml)
- storage_path — user-chosen task storage directory (e.g. ~/.dex/task-db/greg.jsonl)

Backend:
- Project and add_project take name, config_path, storage_path
- gdex must not create, write, or delete config or task storage files
- DexClient passes both paths to every dex invocation

UI (future, under project management): two pickers for config file and storage directory; validate paths exist before save.

Out of scope: auto-generating config.toml under app data; storage-only single-picker registration.
result: Reverted storage-only experiment. Project and add_project keep name, config_path, storage_path; gdex does not create or delete dex config/storage files. DexClient unchanged (both paths per invoke). UI dual-picker deferred to project-management milestone.

name: Plugins + Rust deps
description: bun tauri add store dialog. Cargo: notify, thiserror; confirm serde/serde_json. Capabilities for dialog. Dex binary resolved at startup via std::process::Command (which dex) with result stored in Tauri managed state — handles macOS PATH gotcha. All dex invocations happen Rust-side via std::process::Command; no shell plugin needed.
result: Added tauri-plugin-store and tauri-plugin-dialog (no shell). Cargo: notify 7, thiserror 2; serde/serde_json confirmed. dialog:default capability for folder picker. Dex path resolved at startup via which dex into DexBinaryPath managed state; fails fast if missing. Validation: check, typecheck, fallow:audit, clippy, fmt:rust:check all pass.

name: Tests: public/private slug routing
description:
Lock in slug visibility rules with fixture-based tests (not only live projects.json).

Cover:
- Exact match: public and private both redirect
- Fuzzy (fuzzysort): public typos resolve; typos near private slugs do not
- resolvePath end-to-end for typo → private → not_found
- findFuzzyPublicProject never returns a private project

Done when: tests use a small Project[] fixture and pass without conditional skips.
result: Fixture-based tests in routing.visibility.test.ts; resolvePathWithProjects for injection. Covers exact public/private, fuzzy public, typo→private 404, fuzzy never returns private.

name: Better / UI: Preact + edge-friendly styling
description:
Upgrade the / hub from bare HTML to a proper renderer and design system (edge-friendly).

Direction:
- Preact for the fuzzy-find hub
- Styling: Vanilla Extract (@vanilla-extract/css + @vanilla-extract/vite-plugin) — zero-runtime, build-time CSS; install with `bun i` (dev deps). Do not use Tailwind for this task.
- Verify Vite+ dev/build and Cloudflare Worker bundle after wiring VE

Requirements:
- Polished, minimal UI consistent with a personal project hub
- Works in Cloudflare Workers + Vite+ dev/build story
- Shares projects map with slug redirect routes (reuse `publicProjects` / `searchPublicProjects`)

Depends on: h39pjguq (Fuzzy-find hub at /) — done on main.

Done when:
- / uses Preact + Vanilla Extract
- Dev and production deploy look intentional, not starter-template
result: Hub uses Preact + Vanilla Extract with prerendered /, custom dark UI, combobox a11y, and shared projects catalog with slug redirects. vp build passes (Worker + client).

name: vilos92.com
description:
Umbrella for vilos92.com repo work. Use child tasks for concrete efforts.

Vision:
- vilos92.com/ — simple site with fuzzy find across Greg's GitHub projects
- vilos92.com/<slug> — go straight to the matching GitHub project (short path → repo)

Notes:
- Super minimal surface area; routing/redirect hub, not a full app
- Child tasks: implementation milestones, config (slug → repo map), deploy, etc.
result: vilos92.com hub live: fuzzy find at /, slug redirects and API resolve, Preact + Vanilla Extract UI on Cloudflare Workers.

name: Drag to reprioritize tasks + task deletion
description: Two interaction features: (1) Drag and drop to reprioritize tasks within the current list level. On drop, swap the priority values of the dragged item and the drop target via dex edit <id> -p <n>. Known problem: when multiple tasks share the same priority value (dex defaults new tasks to 1), a swap between two equal-priority tasks is a no-op and visual order is unstable. One possible approach is to normalize priorities to sequential values based on current visual order before the first drag. Before implementing, explore alternatives and discuss the tradeoff with the user. (2) Task deletion — UI implications are non-trivial (must warn about subtask cascade, confirm intent). Deletion UI design should be discussed as part of oobeapzz before implementing — a dex command snippet may be the safer v0 answer. Implement drag-to-reprioritize first; treat deletion as a follow-on pending the oobeapzz design session.

name: Milestone 1: shell + paths consistent
description: Decide Bash vs POSIX sh; fix redirection portability; use /Users/greg.linscheid, avoid hardcoded /Users/...
result: Set brews.sh to bash, fixed portable brew check redirection, and removed hardcoded home path in .zprofile writes.

name: Ingredient page: detail + recipes using it
description:
## Goal
After data is **confirmed** into the library, navigate ingredient → recipes that use it (audit curated graph, not scan-time behavior).

## Today
- Flat `/ingredient` list only; no detail route or “recipes using this” query.

## Work
1. Route e.g. `/ingredient/$ingredientId` + server fn listing recipes with that `ingredient_id`.
2. Links from list → detail → recipe pages.

## Acceptance
- Used ingredients show recipe links; orphans show empty state.
result: Ingredient layout with Outlet, /ingredient/$id detail + recipes list; links from index and recipe detail.

name: v0 integration pass
description: Smoke test the full app end to end. Exact checklist TBD once the backend and UI are in place.

name: Milestone 2: remove eval + add safety/guards
description: Replace eval-based command execution; add strict mode and sensible traps; guard steps that assume tools (pip3/pipx/etc).
result: Removed eval-based installs by switching prompt helper to execute command+args; added strict bash mode; added shell-only helper for pipelines/&&; added python3 guard for copyparty install.

name: DexClient (Rust)
description: src-tauri/src/dex_client.rs: shells out to dex for reads only in v0. Each call passes --config and --storage-path for the active project. Reads: dex list --json (flat array, reconstruct tree client-side via parent_id/children fields) and dex show <id> --json for detail. Define a Task struct matching the JSON shape. DexError via thiserror. Expose: list_tasks, show_task.
result: Implemented dex_client.rs: read-only DexClient shells out to resolved dex binary for list_tasks and show_task with Task/DexError types.

name: Cleanup and polish
description: Full pass over the app since initial creation—UI, code, consistency, dead ends. Greg will review files manually first; scope and subtasks to be added as we go.
result: PR #1 merged to main: lib refactor (@/ aliases, slug-fuzzy, project-search), CI fallow audit, AGENTS validation loop, sync-projects-json skill; CodeRabbit feedback (skill fallow step, text fence, deterministic slug-fuzzy fixtures). vp check, vp test, fallow:audit passed.

name: Introduce Zustand for shared UI state
description: Add Zustand and migrate app state out of hooks and prop drilling. Store shape: projects, activeProjectId, tasks (for active project), selectedTaskId, zoomedTaskId (drill-down context). Move the tasks-changed event subscription into the store so it is not tied to component lifecycle. Clean up ProjectsLayout — it should read from the store rather than threading state through props. Do this before or alongside the detail panel milestone so selectedTaskId is available to both the task list and detail panel without lifting state. Also introduce Zod schemas for all data crossing the Tauri boundary (Task, Project, and any other invoke return types). Schemas live in src/schemas/ (e.g. src/schemas/task.ts, src/schemas/project.ts) using a Schemas. section comment per AGENTS.md file layout conventions. Parse at the call site in taskApi.ts and projectApi.ts — if Rust sends back a shape we don't expect, we catch it immediately at the edge rather than letting bad data propagate into the store.
result: Zod schemas parse Tauri invoke payloads at taskApi/projectApi edges. Zustand appStore holds projects, active project, tasks, loading/error, and task nav; initTasksListener and reloadTasks live in the store. Hooks/views read from store—ProjectMain and ProjectsLayout no longer thread seven props.

name: Import: confirm before DB + Spoonacular
description:
## Goal
Single commit path after human confirm. **Zero** DB writes and **zero** Spoonacular/Claude enrichment during scan.

## Why (read parent `jvprxjqg`)
Turso should only receive imports Greg has audited. Spoonacular + Claude candidate-pick are expensive and pointless if the parse is wrong — run them **only after confirm**, for ingredients actually being saved.

## Today
- Scan: `resolveIngredientsAgainstDb` inserts `ingredient` rows + may Spoonacular-enrich (`ingredient-resolution.ts`).
- Confirm-ish: **Create recipe** already inserts `recipe` + `recipe_ingredient` only (but ingredient IDs must already exist from scan).
- Escape hatch: manual enrich on `/ingredient` list stays.

## Work
1. Scan/extract: structured preview only; optional **read-only** match to existing ingredients for display labels (no insert).
2. Confirm: resolve/create ingredients → Spoonacular for **new** rows only (if key set) → insert recipe + joins; one server fn / transaction preferred.
3. UI: confirm vs discard; drop “new ingredient” badges that implied DB state during scan.
4. **If Greg flags a bad site during this work:** stop coding around it — capture URL + HTML fixture under `test/fixtures/recipes/golden/`, adjust prompts/schemas, run `bun run test:ai:recipe:url:golden`. Collaborate; don’t auto-insert workarounds.

## Acceptance
- `ingredient` row count unchanged after scan, before confirm.
- Spoonacular not called during scan.
- After confirm: same end state as today when key present.
result: Merged PR #5: preview-only extract, confirm-time resolve + Spoonacular, duplicate URL check, transactional persist (ingredient match + recipe insert).

name: Master recipe catalog for goldens
description: After fixture reorg (and likely co-located docs for how to create/run fixtures), add a single master catalog (recipe list) — format TBD: recipe, parse-difficulty score, parsing notes. Catalog may list more recipes than we have fixtures; every fixture MUST reference an entry in this catalog. Bootstrap: scan repo for existing recipe URLs near current fixtures and seed the catalog before adding new URLs from Greg.
result: Added test/fixtures/recipes/catalog.yaml (7 entries: 4 golden, 3 candidate) with parseDifficulty 1–5 and parsingNotes. README documents fields and scale. Golden expected.json uses catalogId; recipe-url-golden.ts resolves canonicalUrl from catalog and validates goldenFixtureBase.
